mcqs.question: Which disk scheduling algorithm is also known as the elevator algorithm?
mcqs.explanation: SCAN algorithm is called elevator algorithm because it moves the disk head in one direction servicing all requests until it reaches the end, then reverses direction - similar to how an elevator works in a building.

mcqs.question: Which file allocation method provides the fastest random access to files?
mcqs.explanation: Contiguous allocation provides the fastest random access because all blocks of a file are stored consecutively, requiring only one seek operation to access any block.

mcqs.question: What is the main disadvantage of linked file allocation?
mcqs.explanation: Linked allocation stores file blocks scattered across the disk with pointers, making random access inefficient as you must traverse all preceding blocks to reach a specific block.

mcqs.question: Which free space management technique uses a bitmap?
mcqs.explanation: Bit vector (also called bitmap) method uses a bitmap where each bit represents a disk block - 0 indicates free and 1 indicates allocated.

mcqs.question: In the SCAN algorithm, what happens after the head reaches the end of the disk?

options.text: It reverses direction and continues servicing
It services the same requests again

mcqs.explanation: After reaching the end of the disk in SCAN algorithm, the head reverses direction and continues servicing requests that were pending in the opposite direction.

mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of RAID Level 1?
mcqs.explanation: RAID Level 1 (mirroring) duplicates data across two or more disks to provide redundancy and fault tolerance, ensuring data is not lost if one disk fails.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a benefit of buffer cache?

options.text: Serves frequently accessed data from memory

mcqs.explanation: Buffer cache reduces disk I/O by caching data in memory, but it does not eliminate the need for disk scheduling which still determines the order of physical disk access requests.

mcqs.question: Swap space is typically located in:
mcqs.explanation: Swap space is located in secondary storage (usually a dedicated partition or file on disk) and is used to extend RAM when physical memory is full.

mcqs.question: In indexed allocation, what is an index block?
mcqs.back: A block that contains pointers to all the data blocks of a file, allowing direct access to any block without traversing other blocks.

options.text: A block that contains pointers to all the data blocks of a file
The first block of every file

mcqs.question: What problem does the C-SCAN algorithm solve that SCAN does not?

options.text: Equal wait time for all requests

mcqs.explanation: C-SCAN provides more uniform wait time by servicing requests only in one direction and then jumping back to the beginning, ensuring all requests at outer tracks wait the same time.

mcqs.question: Which RAID level provides the best fault tolerance with minimum overhead?
mcqs.explanation: RAID 6 provides dual parity, allowing the array to survive up to two simultaneous disk failures with efficient storage overhead compared to full mirroring (RAID 1).

mcqs.question: What is disk fragmentation?

options.text: Scattered free spaces that cannot be used for large files

mcqs.explanation: Disk fragmentation occurs when free disk space becomes scattered in small chunks, making it difficult to allocate contiguous space for new files, degrading performance.

mcqs.question: The SSTF algorithm may cause starvation for:

options.text: Requests near the current head position
Requests far from the current head position

mcqs.explanation: SSTF always selects the nearest request, so requests located far from the current head position may be repeatedly bypassed, leading to starvation.

mcqs.question: What type of file system is commonly used in Windows operating systems?
mcqs.explanation: NTFS (New Technology File System) is the standard file system for Windows NT and later versions, offering features like journaling, encryption, and access control.

mcqs.question: Which disk scheduling algorithm provides the minimum seek time?
mcqs.explanation: SSTF (Shortest Seek Time First) provides the minimum seek time in theory because it always selects the request closest to the current head position.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of disk quota in operating systems?

options.text: To limit the amount of disk space each user can use

mcqs.explanation: Disk quota is a system feature that limits the amount of disk space and/or number of files that users can create, preventing any single user from consuming all available storage.

mcqs.question: What is journaling in file systems?
mcqs.back: Journaling records file system operations in a log before executing them, allowing quick recovery from crashes by replaying or undoing incomplete operations.

options.text: A backup technique for disk files
Recording file system operations in a log before executing them

mcqs.question: Which memory management technique uses swap space?
mcqs.back: Both paging and segmentation can use swap space to temporarily move inactive memory segments or pages to secondary storage when RAM is full.

mcqs.question: What is the main advantage of indexed file allocation over linked allocation?
mcqs.explanation: Indexed allocation supports efficient direct access because all block pointers are stored in the index block, allowing immediate access to any block without traversing the file.

mcqs.question: In a bit vector for free space management, what does a value of 0 represent?
mcqs.explanation: In a bit vector (bitmap), typically 0 represents a free/available block and 1 represents an allocated/used block, though the convention can vary.
